content: Case-Based Reasoning can resolve the longstanding 'noisy novelty' problem of ICM curiosity mechanisms in 2025-era autonomous swarm UAVs: ICM drives agents to explore novel states by rewarding unpredictable, unvisited environments, while CBR filters out valueless novelty (like randomly generated meaningless sensor noise that triggers curiosity) by comparing new unfamiliar observations to stored past cases, only retaining exploration rewards for novel states that are structurally similar to previously useful problem-solving cases. This hybrid framework lets autonomous agents balance open-ended curiosity-driven exploration without wasting computation on deceptive valueless novelty.
